roadmap.binding_source: docs/feature/session-onboarding/design/event-slice-scope.md (RATIFIED 2026-05-24; OQ-E1 enforce·403 / OQ-E2 function / OQ-E3 incremental)
extends: docs/feature/session-onboarding/design/event-model.md (Specs 4/5 reused; addendum: no new domain events)

test_strategy.stack: TypeScript + vitest + Hono. NOT pytest-bdd. No .feature files, no step definitions, no Python scaffolds — wrong for this stack.
test_strategy.strategy_label: Strategy B equivalent — real local in-process transport (the Hono app via app.fetch), costly external boundary (WorkOS /oauth/userinfo + backend /api/orgs) faked by an injected mock `fetch`.
test_strategy.test_home: ui-state/index.test.ts (extended in place — the existing Spec-style describe/it template; all /event-parity scenarios appended).
test_strategy.driving_port: POST /flow/session-onboarding/event (the ACL router, router.ts:201-244). Every scenario enters here; assertions are at the projection / HTTP-status boundary (GET /flow/session-onboarding/projection or the /event response body). No internal-component access.
test_strategy.driven_port: WorkOS /oauth/userinfo + backend /api/orgs(/me)+/api/auth/reissue, exercised through the injected mock `fetch` (deps.request_client). The ONLY test double; a true external boundary (L3/L4).
test_strategy.mandate_7_na: RED tests go RED because the validation behavior is unwritten, NOT because of a missing module — all production modules already exist (router.ts, machine.ts, setup/domain.ts). There is nothing to scaffold; the __SCAFFOLD__/ImportError convention does not apply.
test_strategy.characterization_first: Per Feathers + CLAUDE.md Iron Rule, Slices 1-3 are CHARACTERIZATION written GREEN against current code — they pin currently-untested observable behavior of the existing /event handler so Slices 4-6 can edit it safely. This is the brownfield analog of the walking skeleton.
test_strategy.red_convention: Slices 4-6 are BEHAVIOR CHANGES marked it.skip with a leading `// RED until DELIVER Slice N — <behavior>` comment, encoding the POST-implementation TARGET assertion. The suite stays green; DELIVER un-skips one at a time (Outside-In: un-skip → watch RED → implement minimum → GREEN). Iron Rule: a skipped spec is implemented to green, never weakened to pass.
test_strategy.failure_sim_gate_mechanism: The __force_failure__ + force_reissue_failures harness side-channels are gated by @dashboard-chat/shared-failure-simulation `shouldInject`, which reads a MODULE-SCOPED verdict cache populated by `probe(env, service)`. Tests enable the gate with `probe({ENVIRONMENT:'ci', FAILURE_SIMULATION_ENABLED:'true'}, 'ui-state')` (enableFailureSimulation helper) and the production fail-closed default disables it (disableFailureSimulation helper = `probe({}, 'ui-state')`). afterEach ALWAYS restores the disabled default because the cache is process-global; Vitest's per-file worker isolation plus this reset keep the gate from leaking across files (verified: full ui-state suite 152 pass / 4 skip, no regressions).
test_strategy.error_path_ratio: 9 error/edge scenarios of 14 parity scenarios (~64%) — well above the 40% mandate. (Slice1 2 refusals, Slice2 2 recoverable-error, Slice3 1 refusal, Slice4 1 refusal, Slice5 1 refusal, Slice6 1 refusal + 1 domain-rule contrast.)
test_strategy.kpi_contracts: docs/product/kpi-contracts.yaml NOT present — @kpi observability scenarios skipped (soft gate, warning logged).
test_strategy.story_traceability: docs/feature/session-onboarding/discuss/ NOT present (DESIGN-entry brownfield). ACs derived from DESIGN (event-slice-scope.md §4 G/W/T seeds); story-to-scenario traceability mapping is N/A and skipped per graceful-degradation.

regression_gate_definition.applies_to: every slice (1..6), as the merge prerequisite
steps: cd ui-state && npx vitest run    # full ui-state suite GREEN (incl. the slice's own un-skipped spec); baseline 152 pass / 4 skip
cd ui-state && npx eslint index.test.ts    # 0 errors
./tools/test/test.sh --auto      # merge-queue gate (ruff+pytest on code touch; ui-state vitest unaffected by the --auto selector)
invariants: The 10 pre-existing Specs (1-5, 7, legacy-alias x2) and the Slice 1-3 + Slice 6-contrast characterization tests stay GREEN with ZERO regression — never weakened or re-skipped to make a behavior-change slice pass.

All payload/identity translation lands in session-onboarding/router.ts ONLY — never in the shared orchestrator.ts `send` (machine-agnostic; ADR-028 'no machine knows another'). Tests assert at the HTTP/projection boundary so they don't dictate this, but a fix that leaked translation into the orchestrator would break sibling-machine suites.
The afterEach gate reset (disableFailureSimulation) must remain — without it an enabled scenario leaks the open verdict into the next file.

known_hazard.description: The failure-simulation verdict cache is module-global. Any new test that enables the gate MUST restore it (the file-level afterEach already does). A test that forgets could open the gate for a later file's force-failure assertion. Vitest per-file worker isolation mitigates this across files; the afterEach mitigates it within the file.

phases.name: /event-to-/begin parity — DTO + ACL boundary validation
phases.description: Bring POST /flow/session-onboarding/event to parity with the realigned /begin: zod request DTO, ACL identity translation (flow_id from the verified principal), tag validation against the domain failure vocabulary, and payload well-formedness — all at the router (ACL), none in the domain model (D-E1) beyond export-widening isUnderlyingCauseTag (D-E2). Strict order: characterization (1-3) before behavior change (4-6).

steps.name: Introduce eventRequestSchema (zod DTO) — behavior-preserving
steps.nature: characterization (GREEN now) — the DELIVER refactor must preserve the pinned 400 contract
steps.binding_source: event-slice-scope.md §4 Slice 1; OQ-E3 INCREMENTAL (minimal schema first: flow_id/type presence + passthrough payload)
steps.observable_outcome: A malformed event (no flow_id OR no type) is refused with HTTP 400; a well-formed org submission still reaches ready.
gating_tests: index.test.ts :: Slice 1: a malformed event missing its flow id or type is refused > refuses an org-submission that names no flow (GREEN)
index.test.ts :: Slice 1: ... > refuses an event that names a flow but no event type (GREEN)
index.test.ts :: Slice 1: ... > accepts a well-formed org submission and reaches ready (GREEN)
files_to_touch: ui-state/lib/machines/session-onboarding/router.ts (replace inline body type + manual `if (!flow_id || !type)` with a zod eventRequestSchema; add the session_onboarding.event_received audit log)
steps.note: The characterization tests pin the OBSERVABLE 400 contract (error present, status 400) — deliberately NOT the exact error string, so the DTO refactor may upgrade the body to carry zod `issues` without breaking the test.

steps.name: Characterize retry_clicked over /event at the HTTP layer
steps.nature: characterization (GREEN now) — test-only, no production change
steps.binding_source: event-slice-scope.md §4 Slice 2; event-model.md Phase 3 'Org-create retry'; journey failure_mode jwt_reissue_failed_after_org_create
steps.driving_port: POST /flow/session-onboarding/event (retry_clicked) — driven into error_recoverable via the force_reissue_failures budget side-channel on /begin
steps.observable_outcome: Org-create reissue-budget exhaustion settles to error_recoverable{partial-setup}; each retry_clicked over /event is accepted (200) and the user REMAINS on the recoverable-error screen.
gating_tests: index.test.ts :: Slice 2: retrying after a recoverable org-setup failure keeps the recoverable-error screen > lands on the recoverable-error screen when the org-setup reissue budget is exhausted (GREEN)
index.test.ts :: Slice 2: ... > accepts each retry over /event and keeps the user on the recoverable-error screen (GREEN)
steps.finding: The seed's assertion `error_recoverable -> ... -> error_terminal` and `retry_budget_used_count == 3` are NOT observable at the projection boundary: the projection has NO error_terminal reducer and no terminal event is emitted when the actor escalates, and retry_budget_used_count is actor-internal (absent from the projected context). The characterization pins the actual observable truth (retry accepted, stays recoverable) per the Iron Rule. See distill/upstream-issues.md observability gap U-E1.

steps.name: Characterize the __force_failure__ gate (both arms) over /event
steps.nature: characterization (GREEN now) — test-only, no production change
steps.observable_outcome: Switch OFF (production default): 403 with the gate-disabled message, projection unchanged (needs_org). Switch ON (dev/ci tier + flag): 200, error_recoverable, underlying_cause_tag == 'transient'.
gating_tests: index.test.ts :: Slice 3: the forced-failure side-channel is gated unless the failure-simulation switch is on > refuses a forced failure when the failure-simulation switch is off (GREEN)
index.test.ts :: Slice 3: ... > routes a forced failure into the recoverable-error screen when the switch is on (GREEN)
steps.note: The gate is enabled in-test via probe({ENVIRONMENT:'ci', FAILURE_SIMULATION_ENABLED:'true'}, 'ui-state'); the disabled arm needs no setup (fail-closed default). The needs_org source-state precondition is asserted explicitly so the machine's silent-ignore-elsewhere behavior (event-slice-scope.md §6 risk) is documented, not discovered later.

steps.name: Validate __force_failure__.tag against UnderlyingCauseTag at the ACL — behavior change
steps.binding_source: event-slice-scope.md §4 Slice 4; D-E2 (reuse the domain's closed failure vocabulary)
steps.driving_port: POST /flow/session-onboarding/event (__force_failure__ with an out-of-vocabulary tag), gate ON
steps.observable_outcome: A forced failure whose cause is not in the failure vocabulary -> HTTP 400 (invalid_request); the projection is UNCHANGED (still needs_org, underlying_cause_tag null); no forced-failure event reaches the actor.
gating_tests: index.test.ts :: Slice 4: a forced failure naming an unrecognized cause is refused at the boundary > refuses a forced failure whose cause is not in the failure vocabulary (RED — it.skip)
files_to_touch: ui-state/lib/machines/session-onboarding/router.ts (after the gate passes, validate payload.tag with the imported isUnderlyingCauseTag; invalid/absent -> 400)
ui-state/lib/machines/session-onboarding/setup/domain.ts (export-widen isUnderlyingCauseTag — the ONLY domain touch in the whole slice, an export, not new modeling; D-E2)

steps.name: Derive flow_id from the verified principal (cross-principal guard) — behavior change
steps.binding_source: event-slice-scope.md §4 Slice 5; D-E3; OQ-E1 RATIFIED ENFORCE (403); OQ-E2 RATIFIED FUNCTION (translateWireEvent, not a class)
steps.driving_port: POST /flow/session-onboarding/event with X-User-Id verified-principal header + body flow_id
steps.observable_outcome: A body flow_id that does not belong to the verified principal -> HTTP 403, no event reaches the other flow. The matching-principal happy path -> 200 + ready (Spec 4 behavior, now authorized).
gating_tests: index.test.ts :: Slice 5: an event is accepted only for the verified principal's own flow > refuses an event whose flow belongs to a different principal (RED — it.skip)
index.test.ts :: Slice 5: ... > accepts an event for the verified principal's own flow and reaches ready (RED — it.skip)
files_to_touch: ui-state/lib/machines/session-onboarding/router.ts (read c.get('userId'); compute expected = `session-onboarding:${userId}`; reject mismatch with 403; add the translateWireEvent ACL function returning the typed inbound command the orchestrator's send consumes)
steps.work_item_inside_slice: FE + TS-harness X-User-Id audit (OQ-E1): the acceptance suite fails loudly on any /event client that omits X-User-Id. The current /event Specs (4/5/7) send NO X-User-Id — that is the gap this slice's RED tests expose. The harness (tests/acceptance/user-flow-state-machines/harness/) must be confirmed to send X-User-Id on every /event path during DELIVER.
steps.current_behavior_observed: Today a mismatched flow_id for a NONEXISTENT actor returns 500 (unknown flow_id throw), not 403 — the cross-principal hole is real for an EXISTING sibling actor. The RED test asserts the TARGET 403 (rejected at the ACL before reaching the orchestrator).

steps.name: Payload well-formedness for org_form_submitted at the ACL — behavior change
steps.nature: behavior change (RED for well-formedness; GREEN contrast for the domain rule)
steps.binding_source: event-slice-scope.md §4 Slice 6; D-E1 (the empty-string DOMAIN rule stays on constructOrgName, NOT promoted to the ACL); OQ-E3 INCREMENTAL
steps.driving_port: POST /flow/session-onboarding/event (org_form_submitted with malformed vs domain-invalid payload)
steps.observable_outcome: payload:{} (org_name absent) -> HTTP 400 at the boundary, projection unchanged (needs_org). CONTRAST: payload:{org_name:''} STILL -> 200 + validation_failed{empty} in needs_org (Spec 5, the domain rule, unchanged).
gating_tests: index.test.ts :: Slice 6: a malformed org submission is refused while the empty-name domain rule stays in the model > refuses an org submission that carries no organization name (RED — it.skip)

index.test.ts :: Slice 6: ... > still surfaces the empty-name validation error for an empty organization name (domain rule, unchanged) (GREEN — the contrast, characterizes that the rule was NOT promoted to the ACL)
files_to_touch: ui-state/lib/machines/session-onboarding/router.ts (validate org_form_submitted carries payload.org_name:string at the boundary — presence/type only; the shape rule stays on constructOrgName)
steps.current_behavior_observed: Today payload:{} slips through to the actor and proceeds to creating_org as a silent malformed-command no-op (observed: 200 + state creating_org). The RED test asserts the TARGET 400 + unchanged needs_org. The empty-string contrast already behaves correctly (200 + empty validation error) and stays green.
